woodFarmCodes.title: My Wood Farm Codes
woodFarmCodes.intro: My Wood Farm currently has no active reward codes or public code redemption menu. The official Roblox experience and the Did I Axe developer community are the main places to check for future code releases.

cards.reward: No code rewards are currently available
No previously released public codes are listed
cards.action: Watch the developer community for announcements

cards.reward: The game does not currently list a code redemption feature
cards.action: Future codes will require an in-game redemption menu

woodFarmBeginnerGuide.title: My Wood Farm Beginner Guide
woodFarmBeginnerGuide.intro: Your first goal is to turn trees into wood, sell that wood for cash and reinvest the money into stronger axes and farm expansion. Focus on production upgrades early because your axes can also continue farming wood while you are offline.

steps.title: Start Chopping Trees
steps.description: Equip your starting axe and chop the trees available on your plot. Keep chopping until you have enough wood to make your first useful sale.
steps.tip: Stay close to the trees on your own plot to keep the first session simple.

steps.title: Sell Your Wood
steps.description: Convert the wood you produce into cash. Selling regularly gives you the money needed for axes, upgrades and plot progression.
steps.tip: Do not leave usable wood sitting unsold when you are close to affording an upgrade.

steps.title: Buy a Better Axe
steps.description: Spend your first savings on an axe upgrade that improves your wood production. Stronger axe progression is the main route from slow early chopping to larger amounts of wood.
steps.tip: Prioritize production before saving for expensive late-game axe categories.

steps.title: Repeat the Production Loop
steps.description: Chop more trees with the upgraded axe, sell the additional wood and reinvest the higher income. Each completed cycle should move the farm toward the next upgrade faster.
steps.tip: Compare how quickly each new axe helps you reach the following purchase.

steps.title: Upgrade and Expand the Plot
steps.description: Use cash to improve and expand your farm plot as new progression options become affordable. Plot development works alongside axe upgrades as part of the main game loop.
steps.tip: Keep enough cash available for production upgrades instead of spending everything at once.

steps.title: Use Offline Wood Production
steps.description: Your axes continue farming wood while you are offline. Return after a break, sell the additional production and use the cash to continue upgrading.
steps.tip: Offline farming is especially useful when you cannot stay in an active chopping session.

woodFarmAxeTierList.title: My Wood Farm Axe Tier List
woodFarmAxeTierList.intro: Axes determine how quickly your farm can produce wood during active play and offline farming. Upgrade through the early axe levels first, then target rare, legendary and magical axe categories as your cash production increases.

tiers.label: Best Late-Game
tiers.name: Magical Axes
tiers.description: The highest progression category shown in current My Wood Farm gameplay. These axes are the main long-term target after building a strong cash-producing farm.

tiers.label: Excellent
tiers.name: Legendary Axes
tiers.description: Powerful rare upgrades that provide a major step beyond normal axe progression. Legendary axes are strong targets before moving into magical axe levels.

tiers.label: Strong Progression
tiers.name: Rare Axes
tiers.description: Useful progression upgrades for players who have moved beyond starter equipment but are still building enough income for legendary or magical axes.

tiers.label: Starter
tiers.name: Wooden Axe
tiers.description: The basic starting point for chopping trees and earning initial cash. Replace it after your farm can comfortably afford a stronger production upgrade.

woodFarmMoneyFarming.title: Best Ways to Make Money in My Wood Farm
woodFarmMoneyFarming.intro: The fastest money progression comes from repeatedly converting wood into cash and reinvesting that cash into production. Active chopping works best during short focused sessions, while upgraded axes and offline farming continue supporting longer-term growth.

methods.name: Upgrade Your Axe Early
methods.description: A stronger axe improves the core wood-production loop and helps each future sale contribute more toward the next upgrade.
methods.action: Use early cash for practical axe progression instead of holding it without a target.

methods.name: Actively Chop Trees
methods.description: Continuous chopping provides immediate wood and lets you earn cash without waiting for offline production.
methods.action: Keep moving between available trees and minimize time spent away from production.

methods.name: Sell Wood Regularly
methods.description: Wood only advances your farm after it is converted into cash. Regular sales make upgrades available sooner and keep the progression loop moving.
methods.action: Sell when the current wood amount brings you close to an axe or plot upgrade.

methods.name: Expand and Improve the Plot
methods.description: Plot upgrades and expansion are part of the official progression loop and support the growth of your overall wood farm.
methods.action: Balance plot spending with axe purchases so production continues improving.

methods.name: Use Offline Axe Farming
methods.description: Axes continue farming wood while you are offline, allowing the farm to produce resources between active sessions.
methods.action: Upgrade before logging off so your current axe progression contributes to offline production.

woodFarmTreesAndValues.title: My Wood Farm Trees, Stumps and Wood Values
woodFarmTreesAndValues.intro: My Wood Farm begins with regular trees and gradually introduces stronger targets such as stumps and giant trees. Stronger axes and a larger production setup reduce chopping time and help turn each farming cycle into more wood and cash.

rows.notes: The main target for starting the chop, collect, sell and upgrade loop.

rows.selling_value: Improved cash return per completed target
rows.notes: Stumps are designed for stronger axe progression and take more production power than regular trees.
A long-duration target that benefits from placing more axes and improving total farm output.

woodFarmUpgradeOrder.title: Best My Wood Farm Upgrade Order
woodFarmUpgradeOrder.intro: The most efficient progression keeps cash moving back into upgrades that increase wood production. Compare purchases by measuring how much extra cash they generate instead of buying every available upgrade immediately.

steps.action: Chop regular trees, collect the wood and sell it for cash.
steps.why: This establishes the basic production rate used to judge every later upgrade.
steps.spend_rule: Complete several sales before making the first major purchase.

steps.action: Upgrade the axe that is actively producing wood before spending heavily on extra space.
steps.why: Faster or stronger axes complete tree targets sooner and increase cash earned during each session.
steps.spend_rule: Prioritize upgrades that create an immediate increase in wood production.

steps.action: Fill useful positions on the current plot so multiple axes can contribute to production.
steps.why: Additional axes increase total active output and continue contributing to offline wood production.
steps.spend_rule: Use existing plot space before paying for expansion that would remain empty.

steps.priority: Purchase more land when the current area is full
steps.action: Open additional plot space and place new axes without blocking access to trees or the selling route.
steps.why: Expansion raises the number of production positions available for a larger farm.
steps.spend_rule: Expand when the next axe cannot be placed efficiently on the current plot.

steps.priority: Move into magical and legendary axe progression
steps.action: Use high-tier axes and a larger setup against durable stumps and giant trees.
steps.why: Late-game targets require more chopping power and reward farms built around stronger axes.
steps.spend_rule: Calculate payback time as upgrade cost divided by the extra cash earned per minute.

woodFarmPlotExpansion.title: My Wood Farm Plot Expansion and Layout Guide
woodFarmPlotExpansion.intro: Plot expansion is a core part of My Wood Farm progression. Additional land becomes most valuable when the existing plot is already producing efficiently and the new area can immediately support more axes.

items.title: Expansion Currency and Progression
items.content: Plot expansion is funded with cash earned by selling wood. Continue completing tree cycles and reinvesting the sale income until the next expansion purchase becomes available.

items.title: Best Time to Expand
items.content: Expand after the useful axe positions on the current plot are occupied. Buying land too early leaves cash tied up in empty space instead of increasing wood output.

items.title: Early Plot Layout
items.content: Keep axes close to regular tree targets and maintain a clear route for collecting and selling wood. Group the first production positions together so upgrades are easy to monitor.

items.title: Expanded Farm Layout
items.content: Use newly opened outer space for additional axes while keeping the center clear for movement and larger targets. Build in compact production groups instead of spreading single axes across the entire plot.

items.title: Stump and Giant Tree Area
items.content: Reserve an open section for durable stumps and giant trees. These targets work best with several upgraded axes contributing from nearby positions.

items.title: Expansion Purchase Checklist
items.content: Before buying land, confirm that the current plot is filled, enough cash remains for another axe and the new space can be used immediately. Expansion should increase production rather than only increase farm size.

woodFarmOfflineEarnings.title: My Wood Farm Offline Earnings and Latest Updates
woodFarmOfflineEarnings.intro: Offline production lets the farm keep generating wood between play sessions. A larger collection of upgraded axes improves the value of each return, while the official Roblox experience and Did I Axe community provide the main locations for game changes and announcements.

cards.title: Offline Wood Production
cards.value: Axes continue farming wood while the player is offline
cards.details: Place and upgrade productive axes before leaving so the farm is prepared to generate wood during the break.

cards.title: Offline Earnings Formula
cards.value: Offline wood gained = wood after returning minus wood before leaving
cards.details: Divide the wood gained by the number of offline minutes or hours to calculate the farm's average passive production rate.

cards.title: Estimated Cash Value
cards.value: Estimated cash = offline wood gained multiplied by cash earned per wood
cards.details: Use the current selling return shown during a normal sale to estimate how much the offline production cycle is worth.

cards.title: Best Before-Logout Routine
cards.value: Upgrade, place axes, record wood and then leave
cards.details: Spend available cash on useful production improvements, confirm every axe is placed and record the starting wood total before ending the session.

cards.title: Best Return Routine
cards.details: Claim the produced wood, convert it into cash and reinvest the earnings into the next axe, production improvement or plot expansion.

cards.title: Official Update Tracking
cards.value: Roblox experience page and Did I Axe community
cards.details: Check these sources for changes involving axes, trees, production balance, plot expansion and other newly added systems.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about My Wood Farm

questions.question: What is My Wood Farm?
questions.answer: My Wood Farm is an idle woodcutting simulator on Roblox where you chop trees for wood, sell wood for cash, upgrade axes, expand your plot and keep earning while offline as your axes continue farming.

questions.question: Are there any My Wood Farm codes?
questions.answer: My Wood Farm currently has no active reward codes or public code redemption menu. New codes, when added by the Did I Axe developers, will appear inside the game and through the official Roblox experience page.

questions.question: Do axes keep farming in My Wood Farm while you are offline?
questions.answer: Yes. In My Wood Farm your placed axes continue farming wood while you are offline, so returning after a break lets you sell the extra wood and reinvest the cash into more upgrades.

questions.question: What is the best axe in My Wood Farm?
questions.answer: The strongest long-term target in My Wood Farm is the magical axe category, reached after progressing through wooden, rare and legendary axes. Early on, any affordable axe upgrade that raises wood production is the best choice.

questions.question: How do I make money fast in My Wood Farm?
questions.answer: The fastest money in My Wood Farm comes from chopping trees, selling wood regularly and reinvesting the cash into axe and production upgrades, then letting offline axe farming add extra wood between sessions.

questions.question: Is My Wood Farm free to play on Roblox?
questions.answer: Yes. My Wood Farm is free to play on Roblox. You can join through the official My Wood Farm Roblox experience page and follow the Did I Axe community for updates.

cta.title: Ready to Start Your Wood Farm?
cta.description: Join the Did I Axe Roblox group for updates, or jump straight into My Wood Farm and start chopping trees.
